other_observers: Cole Wild; first found by someone attending the RMBO picnic
description: the bird was about the size of a Ring-billed Gull, maybe slightly larger. It was also similar in shape, but with broader based, broader, proportionally longer wings and a heavier build. The bill looked fairly large for a Jaeger, and was pale based with a dark tip on the outer third of the bill. The overall shape looked heavy for a Jaeger, with a bulky build and relatively large head.  The upperparts of the bird were medium chocolate brown, with no rufous or buffy fringes on the mantle. The cap was a bit contrastingly darker than the nape, which in turn was contrastingly slightly paler than the cap and back. The uppertail coverts were mostly white with some small brown checks. The tail was fairly square for a jaeger, with barely noticeable extension of the two central tail feathers.  The bird had a medium brown "hood" that was slightly paler brown on the throat and upper chest. The belly was whitish with a pale brown tinge. The undertail coverts were strongly barred with medium brown.  The upper wings were the same medium chocolate brown as the back. The outer primaries had white shafts; at it's closest approach I was able to count at least 4-5 white primary shafts. The primaries themselves were uniformly dark from above.  The underwings were checked with whitish, though medium brown was the slightly predominate color. The base of the primaries were white. I was unable to observe a "double flash" in the primaries below, but was assured by an observer who saw the bird much closer than I did that it was there.Behaviors: The bird was mostly observed flying around low over the water, where it flew with a direct, almost falcon-like flight, continually harassing Ring-billed Gulls and at one point a Laughing Gull. After harassing the gulls for a while it flew off to the north, then gained a significant amount of altitude. For the next ten minutes or so it soared in circles at a constant elevation, rarely flapping. It then rapidly descended to the reservoir and after flying low over the surface for a minute or so landed on the water.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenal light phase
